Introduction
OneTrust
- a tool that assesses third-party applications.
Step 1 - Risk profile identification(Risk Questionnaire - Supplier Information, Personal Data, AI, Risk, Impact Questions) Result: Critical, High, Moderate, Low
Step 2 - Cybersecurity Audit is triggered after risk is identified(Questionnaire - General Information, Access Control, etc.). Score: Approved, Conditionally Approved(Supplier should work on the conditional plan), Rejected.
Vendor Level (Risk Level) is stored in the Engagement overview.
Signed/Not signed will be added.
PICASso system
Release - Cybersecurity Residual Risks tab → 3d Party Suppliers & SE Bricks section → TPS Product sub-section
TPS Product/Component name and Third Party Supplier Name fields are manually entered, which causes discrepancies - Need to replace with the lookup
Functional Requirements
From the OneTrust platform, the PICASso needs to receive the following:

PICASso Requirements:
Type
Scenario
Expectations (Result, output, logic, etc.)
Third Party Supplier Name
The user will enter the name for the Third Party Supplier
PICASSO must show the name of the relevant vendor as per the entered text based on the OneTrust Input File
TPS Product / Component Name
The user will select the TPS Product / Component Name
PICASSO must show the vendor engagements relevant for the selected vendor in a dropdown based on the OneTrust Input File
Select IRA Rating
The IRA rating must be populated automatically
PICASSO must set the IRA rating based on the OneTrust Input file.
In case the IRA rating has not been generated in OneTrust, PICASSO must setup this field to a default value
Third Party Supplier Assessment Overall Maturity Level
The Overall Maturity Level  must be populated automatically
PICASSO must set the overall maturity level based on the OneTrust Input file.In case Overall Maturity Level has not been generated in OneTrust, PICASSO must setup this field to a default value
Signed Third Party Products & Services Agreement
This field must be populated automatically
PICASSO must set the field based on the OneTrust Input file.In case this field has not been populated in OneTrust, PICASSO must setup this field to a default value
Supplier ID
This field must be populated automatically
PICASSO must set the value of this field based on the corresponding value in One Trust flat file for the supplier or vendor that was chosen.
Engagement ID
This field must be populated automatically
PICASSO must set the value of this field based on the corresponding value in One Trust flat file for the engagement that was chosen.
Supplier Status
This field must be populated automatically
PICASSO must set the value of this field based on the corresponding value in One Trust flat file for the vendor that was chosen.
Engagement Status
This field must be populated automatically
PICASSO must set the value of this field based on the corresponding value in One Trust flat file for the engagement that was chosen.
IRA Rating ID
This field must be populated automatically
Not available in instance. Clarification needed.
Overall Maturity Level Value ID
This field must be populated automatically
Not available in instance. Clarification needed.
Not all suppliers of Schneider Electric exist in OneTrust - need to create a request on the Assessment for 3rd party supplier ---- If the Supplier cannot be found, a link to the form in OneTrust can be used to declare this supplier on the risk supplier identification.
To check once per
day
whether we have actual data from the OneTrust
OR get a list of the updates from OneTrust, check which releases were impacted by these updates, and notify users about updates(can be shown in the scope of the Release notes, provide all changes on one screen)
Use cases of showing new functionality:
Completely new release - show new functionality
Completed releases - create a new release on the completed releases(clone release) - map previously provided data to the new releases. Notify users about updated functionality to review and update.
Users should provide confirmation that mapped data is correct
. Update data in the background query
In progress releases:
If data has not been provided yet, nothing should happen
If data has been provided,
Users should provide confirmation that mapped data is correct.
Update data in the background query
Freeze updates before the FCSR Review stage(on the
Security & Privacy Readiness Sign Off)
and for completed Releases.
The PICASso should store
updates of the TPS Products  on the PICASso side (both inherited data from the OneTrust and modified on the PICASso)
To Do Action:
Data clean up, make a review, and create a mapping between the data entered in PICASso and the OneTrust standards.
How many releases are completed and are in progress where
TPS PRODUCT is specified? How many TPS PRODUCTs are added to these releases?  -----  Product Id, Release Id, Release status, Number of TPS products

Jira
9f9c65ab-b064-3ac4-86d2-4b1256ef112c
PIC-8768
High Level Integration Architecture
Integration Requirements
Type
High Level
Requirement
Requirement
Scheduled File Export
OneTrust generates a file with the appropriate format consumable by Sharepoint and delivers the file on a daily basis (weekday basis), using the newly created engagement or supplier data or other updated data.
Requirement
Fixed File Format
The file generated from OneTrust must follow a fixed schema with defined column names, types, and value formats and in a format that is consumable by Sharepoint.
Requirement
Data Filters
Supplier Category (Engagements) field to be used as filter in report to be exported. List of categories to be used have been provided.
Requirement
Authentication
Client ID and Client Secret to be provided by the client for SharePoint integration.
Assumption
Sharepoint Setup
SharePoint site URL and document library path must be configured securely in OneTrust based on SE guidance. Assumes the client will maintain a reachable, write-accessible SharePoint endpoint with correct permissions and folder path.
Assumption
Defined schedule
Assumes only one export file will be generated and uploaded. PICASSO will ingest only one file per cycle.
Assumption
Outbound Integration
The integration is outbound-only; OneTrust does not receive feedback or updates from Sharepoint.
Constraint
CSV Format Only
Integration is limited to CSV format (UTF‑8, comma-delimited); no support for JSON or other formats unless explicitly changed.
Constraint
No Partial Uploads
File uploads must be complete and must not be visible to the target system during transfer.
Requirement
File Naming Convention
A consistent naming convention to be used which uses the date of file generation also in the file name to distinguish different files.
Requirement
Attribute Availability
All required attributes (including custom ones) must exist in OneTrust instance before generating report.
Assumption
Connectivity
Assumes OneTrust can reach Microsoft server without network/firewall restrictions.
Functional Aspect
Functional Aspect
Description, purpose, impact, notes, details, etc.
Common Issue
If the export file is not generated or fails validation, no file will be uploaded to Sharepoint. PICASSO will not ingest any data, and the previous day’s data will remain unchanged. Integration team should check OneTrust logs and retry manually if needed.
Scheduling
The export job in OneTrust is scheduled daily (weekday basis). Any changes to timing (e.g., due to holidays or maintenance) must be coordinated with PICASSO to avoid missed pickups.
Functional Impact
If the Client Secret is rotated without updating the credential in OneTrust, the upload will fail. Credential updates must be tracked and tested in advance.
Triggers:
Definition of Trigger
Notes
Daily Export Schedule
Triggers the integration to run once per day (weekday) and generate the export file.
Vendor Category Filter
Includes only vendors that match the approved category list; others are excluded.
Vendor Status Filter
Includes vendors marked as "Active" in OneTrust; excludes "Archived" or "Inactive" vendors.
Engagement Status Filter
Includes engagements with status "Active" or "Under Progress"; excludes others unless full snapshot is required.
Error and Validation Handling
Error Condition
Handling/Message
Manual Handling
SharePoint upload failed due to incorrect credentials
File upload to SharePoint failed. Please verify Client ID, Client Secret, and permissions, then retry.
Update credentials in OneTrust and manually trigger export job.
No export file generated from OneTrust
No file available for upload. Previous day’s data will remain unchanged.
Check OneTrust logs, resolve issue, and manually re-run export.
File format validation failed in PICASSO
File rejected due to schema mismatch.
Correct format in OneTrust export configuration and re-upload.
SharePoint site unreachable
Unable to connect to SharePoint site.
Confirm site availability, network access, and permissions; retry after resolution.
Validation Condition
Handling/Message
Manual Handling
Vendor selected
Engagement dropdown enabled and filtered by chosen vendor
Verify vendor exists in PICASSO backend
Status is Pending/Inactive
Placeholder values for IRA, Maturity Level and Agreement are filled and the fields are greyed out; show Status field. Request raised to complete the pending engagement or record.
Update missing fields in OneTrust and finish the pending engagement or record. Then re-export.
Engagement selected
Auto-populate IRA Rating, Overall Maturity Level, and Signed Agreement fields for Active or completed status.
If auto-fill fails, check OneTrust export and PICASSO mapping
Question
Answer
Which fields need to be mapped as required from OneTrust?
Which fields does PICASso need to transform after receiving? How should it be mapped?
How ofthen OneTrust will share Excel files with data?
Once per day
If the API will be on the OneTrust side and PICASso will use this then the OneTrust API should be secured. Does OneTrust API has any security mechanism now?
How we should get the updated data?
How OneTrust manages updates of the 3rd Party fields?
What should happen once the request for the new supplier is created on the OneTrust?
To validate whether the Third Party Supplier Name should be selected first. After that, the Product/Component Name will be selected based on the supplier chosen (will be chosen automatically once there is only one Product Component)
If the Product is selected first, then the Supplier will be chosen automatically
